sections.title: What are conjunctions and linkers?
sections.content:
**Conjunctions** join clauses or words inside a sentence:
• *and, but, or, because, when, although*

**Linkers** connect separate sentences:
• *however, therefore, for example, moreover*

sections.title: Coordinating conjunctions
sections.content:
**And / but / or / so / yet** go **between** two main clauses:

• I called **and** she answered.
• He tried **but** he failed.
• Stay **or** leave.
• It was late **so** we went home.

sections.title: Subordinating conjunctions
sections.content:
**Because / although / when / if / while / whether** start a dependent clause. Either order works:

• *Because it was late, we left.*
• *We left because it was late.*
• *I don't know whether he agreed.*

sections.title: Linkers
sections.content:
Linkers start a **new sentence** and are followed by a **comma**:

• *It was hard. **However,** we succeeded.*
• *He was late. **Therefore,** we started without him.*
• *I like fruit, **for example** apples.*

sections.title: Contrast: but vs although vs however
sections.content:
Three ways to say the same thing:

• **But** (coordinating): *It was cold **but** we went out.*
• **Although** (subordinating): ***Although** it was cold, we went out.*
• **However** (linker): *It was cold. **However,** we went out.*

**Whereas / while** compare two things: *She likes tea **whereas** he prefers coffee.*

sections.title: Cause and result
sections.content:
**Cause:** *because / since / as* (reason)
**Result:** *so / therefore* (what happened next)

• *Because it rained, we stayed.*
• *It rained **so** we stayed.*
• *It rained. **Therefore,** we stayed.*

sections.title: Time: when, while, during
sections.content:
• **When** = at that moment: *I'll call you **when** I arrive.*
• **While** + clause = during: *I read **while** she cooked.*
• **During** + noun only: ***during** the meeting* (not ~~during I waited~~)

sections.title: The biggest mistake: double linking
sections.content:
Never combine a conjunction **and** a linker for the same idea:

• **Wrong:** ~~Because it rained, **so** we stayed.~~
• **Right:** Because it rained, we stayed. **Or:** It rained **so** we stayed.

• **Wrong:** ~~Although she tried, **but** she failed.~~
• **Right:** Although she tried, she failed. **Or:** She tried **but** she failed.

sections.title: Reference
sections.content:
Full lists by category (essay linkers too): [[reflinkers|Linkers and Conjunctions]] in **Reference**.

[[ref|Reference lists]]

questions.question: Which is correct?

options.a: Because it rained, we stayed at home.
options.b: Because it rained, so we stayed at home.
options.c: Because it rained, therefore we stayed.
options.d: It rained, because we stayed at home.

questions.explanation: Don't use because and so (or therefore) in the same sentence. Use one: Because it rained, we stayed. Or: It rained so we stayed.

questions.question: Which is correct?

options.a: Although he tried, but he failed.
options.b: Although he tried, however he failed.
options.d: Although he tried, and he failed.

questions.explanation: Don't use although and but (or however) together. Use one: Although he tried, he failed. Or: He tried but he failed.

questions.question: Which linker fits? 'It was expensive. _____, we bought it.'
questions.explanation: However shows contrast (expensive vs we bought it). Therefore would mean 'because it was expensive we bought it' which is odd.

questions.question: Which fits? 'I'll call you _____ I arrive.'
questions.explanation: When = at the time (I arrive). While is for two ongoing actions; during needs a noun (during the meeting).

questions.question: Which fits? 'She likes tea, _____ he prefers coffee.' (contrast)
questions.explanation: Whereas (or while) compares two contrasting facts inside one sentence: She likes tea, whereas he prefers coffee.

questions.question: Which fits? 'I don't know _____ she agreed.'

options.d: Both a and b are correct.

questions.explanation: Whether and if can both introduce an indirect yes/no question (I don't know whether/if she agreed). Whether is often preferred in formal writing.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Because it was late, so we went home.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Because it was late, we went home.
It was late so we went home.
Because it was late, we went home
It was late so we went home
questions.explanation: Don't use because and so together. Use one or the other.

questions.question: Wrong: 'Although she was tired, but she continued.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Although she was tired, she continued.
She was tired but she continued.
Although she was tired, she continued
She was tired but she continued
questions.explanation: Don't use although and but together.

questions.question: Fill in: It was difficult. _____, we managed to finish. (contrast)
answers: However
questions.explanation: However shows contrast (difficult vs we managed).

questions.question: Fill in: He didn't study. _____, he failed the exam. (result)
answers: Therefore

questions.question: Fill in: I was reading _____ she was cooking. (at the same time)
answers: while

questions.question: Wrong: 'Because he was ill, therefore he stayed in bed.' Correct the sentence.
answers: Because he was ill, he stayed in bed.
He was ill so he stayed in bed.
Because he was ill, he stayed in bed
He was ill so he stayed in bed
questions.explanation: Don't use because and therefore together.

questions.question: Fill in: We need to hurry _____ we miss the train. (negative purpose / result to avoid)
answers: or
otherwise
or we will
otherwise we will
questions.explanation: Or (we'll) miss = otherwise we'll miss. Shows negative result to avoid.

questions.question: Fill in: The film was long. _____, it was very good.
answers: However

questions.question: Fill in: _____ you see him, tell him to call me.
answers: When
questions.explanation: When = at the time you see him.

questions.question: Fill in: I don't know _____ she's coming. (introducing a yes/no alternative – one word)
answers: whether
if
questions.explanation: Whether and if can both introduce an indirect yes/no question (whether/if she's coming or not).

questions.question: Fill in: _____ it was expensive, we decided to buy it. (contrast)
answers: Although
questions.explanation: Although introduces a contrast (expensive vs we bought it).
